data.title: Trader Eugene: I have no intention of buying the dip after BTC fell below $100,000; I will wait for the $90,000 range.
data.description: Trader Eugene wrote that BTC's drop below $100,000 is significant for two reasons: first, the last support level of the high-cycle bullish structure was breached, with the 50-week trendline being lost for the first time since 2022; second, the $100,000 level, a strong psychological barrier, has transformed from support into resistance. He emphasized that against this backdrop, he has "no intention of buying the dip" and considers the $90,000 area as the next focus, with capital protection being the primary strategy at present.

data.title: XRP ETF Fails to Bump Bulls as Ripple-Linked Token Plunges 7.3% Amid BTC Selloff
data.description:
The much-anticipated XRP exchange-traded fund (ETF) has failed to provide the expected boost to bullish investors, as Ripple-linked tokens experienced a significant decline of 7.3%. This drop comes in the wake of a broader selloff in Bitcoin, which has negatively impacted the overall cryptocurrency market and contributed to the downward trend of XRP's value. Despite hopes that the ETF would attract new capital and drive prices higher, market conditions have proven challenging for XRP and its investors.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2025/11/14/xrp-etf-fails-to-bump-bulls-as-ripple-linked-token-plunges-7-3-amid-btc-selloff

data.title: Czech National Bank Dips Into Bitcoin — A Small $1M Test Portfolio With Big Implications
data.description:
The Czech National Bank has taken a significant step by investing in Bitcoin, allocating a modest sum of $1 million to create a test portfolio. This initiative, although relatively small in the context of the bank's overall financial strategy, carries substantial implications for the future of cryptocurrency in the country's financial landscape and could influence the approach of other central banks toward digital currencies. By exploring the potential of Bitcoin, the Czech National Bank is not only testing the waters of this volatile market but also signaling a progressive attitude towards integrating new financial technologies and assets into its operations.
source: https://blocknews.com/czech-national-bank-dips-into-bitcoin-a-small-1m-test-portfolio-with-big-implications/

data.title: A "whale that borrowed 66,000 ETH" purchased 19,508 ETH from Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a "whale that borrowed 66,000 ETH" purchased 19,508 ETH from Binance, worth $61.03 million, and deposited it into Aave V3. Subsequently, the whale borrowed $170 million in USDT from Aave V3 and deposited it into Binance. Currently, the whale holds a total of 405,252 ETH, worth $1.28 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $870 million yesterday, the second highest in history.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $870 million yesterday (November 13th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF (BTC), with a net outflow of $318 million. BTC's historical total net inflow has reached $1.672 billion. This was followed by the BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a single-day net outflow of $257 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $64.252 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $130.536 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.67%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $59.344 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $260 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $260 million yesterday (November 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $137 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $13.622 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a single-day net outflow of $67.9099 million. ETHE's historical total net outflow has reached $4.881 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $20.296 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 5.42%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $13.31 billion.

data.title: Dragonfly Partner: The current bear market is a piece of cake compared to 2022.
data.description: Haseeb, Managing Partner of Dragonfly, wrote on the X platform that the current bear market may be the "easiest" bear market yet. Many people seem to have forgotten what 2022 was like: Luna crashed, then 3AC, then FTX, then Genesis, BlockFi, Axie, NFTs—almost everything teetered on the brink of collapse, like a house of cards. Then, after all that collapsed, banks failed, stablecoins went detached from their pegs, and Gary Gensler attempted to destroy almost every company in the industry. In comparison, the current market situation is "a piece of cake" compared to 2022; although prices have fallen, the fundamentals remain strong.

data.title: Bitfarms Says AI Could Outperform Bitcoin Mining as It Begins 2026–2027 Wind-Down
data.description:
Bitfarms will fully wind down its Bitcoin mining business by 2027 and pivot into high-performance AI infrastructure after reporting deeper quarterly losses.

The post Bitfarms Says AI Could Outperform Bitcoin Mining as It Begins 2026–2027 Wind-Down first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/11/14/bitfarms-says-ai-could-beat-bitcoin/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=bitfarms-says-ai-could-beat-bitcoin
